titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L704
GA L704
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Available for noncommercial re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators and the subsequent derivative is licensed as ShareAlike (Creative Commons by-nc-sa)

projectDesc.p: This transcription was initially made by the Byzantine Text of John Project in 2004-7, led by Roderic L. Mullen, and later adopted for the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John.

The IGNTP transcription file was duplicated for the standalone version of the Byzantine Text of John on 9.7.2014. This means that there may be slight discrepancies between the 2007 file used to prepared the Byzantine apparatus and the 2014 transcription in the present file.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 W1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-A number: L 704 (Athos, Koutloumousiou 90) Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1John begins folio 1, note- folio numbers here are page numbers-- Ρp. 31-32, Jn 5.40-16.2 and 6.14-17, are supplement in a later handΤranscribed by: RLMΤranscription begun: 20.06.02Τranscription finished: 08.11.02Τransference to Collate by: RLMΤransference to Collate begun: 20.06.02Τransference to Collate finished: 08.11.02File date and time: Ρlaced in Level1: RLMLevel1 file date and time: 28.10.02Ρlaced in Level2: read a second time and compared with the collation of Johannes Karavidopoulos. Ηoly Week and Menologion read in MuensterΡlaced in Level2 by: RLMLevel2 file date and time: 15.11.02Τhese files were compared and differences resolved by:Ρlaced in Level3:Ρlaced in Level3 by:Final transcription file date and time: Page layout added by: acmStart date: 3.9.13 End date: 10.9.13Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks:
1. συναξαριον i: πασχα to πεντηκοστη - ευαγγελια κατα ιωαννην - S1 W1 D1 - S1 W8 D1 Easter to Pentecost

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday

ab.note: For the rest of this lection see supplement.

div.note: For the beginning of this lection see supplement.
John 6,35-39 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D6)
John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman

ab.note: The lectionary heading in the top margin is probably for May 21st: του εν αγιοις κωνσταντινουπολεως και ελενης. The lectionary incipit follows: ειπεν αμην.
The lectionary heading in the top margin is unidentified: εκ εγκαινια. The lectionary incipit follows: τω καιρω εγενετο.
The lectionary heading here is probably for September 13th: εις την προσκυ(νησιν) των τιμιων ξυλων.
The lectionary incipit in the top margin is probably for September 13th: ειπεν ο κς̅ ο φιλων.
The lectionary heading in the top margin is probably for September 14th: εις την ορθρον της υψωσεως. The lectionary incipit follows: ειπεν πε̅ρ δοξασον.

div.note: John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)

ab.note: The text contains a reference to locate the reading for S1 W7 D3: John 16,2b-13a.
The text contains a reference to locate the reading for S1 W7 D4: John 16,15-23.
The text contains a reference to locate the reading for S1 W7 D6: John 17,18-26.
The text contains a reference to locate the reading for S1 W8 D1a: John 20,19-23.

div.note: John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτουργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
This lection does not have a heading and carries straight on from Matthew.
This lection does not have a heading and carries straight on from the previous lection. It is also written on P 293.
This lection is first written on P 279. Its inclusion here is out of lection order although here it has a lection heading.

ab.note: The lectionary heading here is for S1 W6 D6 (John 14,1-11a): τη παρασκευη της [num]ϛ[\num] εβδομαδος. The lectionary incipit follows: ειπεν τοις εαυτοις μαθηταις μη ταρασσεσθω.
The lectionary heading here is probably for M1 D2a: του αγιου κυριου. The lectionary incipit is in the top margin: ειπεν εγω ειμι η αμπελος.
The lectionary heading here is unidentified: του αγιου μωκιου. The lectionary incipit is in the top margin: ειπεν τοις εαυτου μαθηταις καθως ηγαπησεν.
The lectionary heading in the top margin is for S1 W7 D3: τη [num]γ[\num] της [num]ν[\num]. The lectionary incipit follows: ειπεν τοις εαυτου μαθηταις ερχεται.
The lectionary heading here is for S1 W7 D4: τη [num]δ[\num] της [num]ν[\num]. The lectionary incipit is in the top margin: ειπεν τοις εαυτου μαθηταις παντα οσα εχει.

The lectionary heading here is unidentified: της συνοδ(ιας) ευαγγελιον. The lectionary incipit is in the top margin: τω καιρω επαρας ο ις̅ τους οφθαλμους αυτων εις τοις μαθηταις αυτου ειπεν πε̅ρ αγιε.

The lectionary heading here is for S1 W7 D6: τη παρασκευη της [num]ν[\num]. The lectionary incipit is in the top margin: τω καιρω επαρας ο ις̅ τους οφθαλμους εις τοις μαθηταις αυτου ειπεν καθως συ πε̅ρ εμε απεστειλας.

div.note: John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)

ab.note: These 4 lines contain instructions for locating this complete lection in the Gospels of the Ρassions although there are enough verses repeated here to warrant a transcription.

div.note: This lection does not have a heading and carries straight on from Matthew.
The heading instructs the user to read this lection on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.

ab.note: The text contains a reference to locate the reading for R9: John 20,19-31.
